1.name: Everyman — grey tee + jeans
1.iconic: plain light-grey crew-neck short-sleeve T-shirt + classic blue jeans, barefoot
1.subject: a Latino man with warm brown skin (clearly brown, not pale or white), short dark straight hair
1.weather: Days 1-3 overcast, no sun, cool, not dark; warms from Day 4

1.title: I wake up here
1.arc_beat: Wake -> confusion -> explore -> first clue (the paper) -> false-hope light -> dead-end cliff -> hears something -> terror.

pieces.prompt: (scenery cold-open: stormy grey jungle island in a churning ocean; NO person; shared no-swap clip)

pieces.caption: Where... where am I?
pieces.prompt: (swap clip: the wake — overhead push-in, lying flat, gasps awake)

pieces.caption: My head... what happened to me?
pieces.prompt: (swap clip: seated, dazed, rubbing temple, looking around)

pieces.caption: Wait... what's that on the ground?
pieces.prompt: 1:1 cinematic HIGH-ANGLE shot looking straight DOWN at the damp jungle FLOOR from above. The ground fills almost the entire frame — wet green leaves, ferns, dark soil, twigs and fallen foliage. Lying in the CENTER of the frame on the ground is a single ripped, half-shredded scrap of old paper — torn jagged edges, badly crumpled and creased, water-stained, smudged with dirt and mud, yellowed and weathered, partly buried in the wet leaves — sharp and in clear focus, the obvious main subject. At the very TOP EDGE of the frame, small and seen from above (a foreshortened top-down view of his head and shoulders and his two bare feet standing on the ground), is a Latino man with warm brown skin, short dark straight hair, plain light-grey crew-neck short-sleeve T-shirt, looking down at the paper — he occupies only the top portion of the image, the ground and paper dominate. Top-down bird's-eye perspective. NO horizon, NO sky, NO distant jungle, NO standing-up portrait — the camera points at the GROUND. Soft flat overcast daylight, cool, not dark, shallow depth of field on the paper, hyperrealistic, 8K.

pieces.caption: What is this...? Who wrote this?
pieces.prompt: (swap clip: notices, picks up, reads the shredded paper, confused)

pieces.caption: Hello?! Is anyone out here?!
pieces.prompt: (swap clip: walking toward camera through jungle, scanning, confused)

pieces.caption: There -- light. Maybe that's a way out.
pieces.prompt: 1:1 cinematic. Seen from behind and to the side (over-the-shoulder), a Latino man with warm brown skin (clearly brown, not pale or white), short dark straight hair, plain light-grey crew-neck short-sleeve T-shirt, classic blue jeans, barefoot, stands in dense dark tropical jungle facing AWAY from the camera toward a bright glowing OPENING ahead of him -- a gap in the thick dark foliage where pale bright overcast daylight and a hint of distant sea shine through, much brighter than the gloomy jungle around him. He faces the light, his head turned toward the opening (we mostly see the back and side of his head, NOT his full face), as if drawn toward a way out. Strong contrast between the dark green jungle and the glowing gap, no sun, no golden light, cool tones, soft mist, no animals. Hyperrealistic, 8K.

pieces.caption: No. No, no... it's just ocean. There's nothing.
pieces.prompt: 1:1 cinematic shot from BEHIND. A Latino man with warm brown skin (clearly brown, not pale or white), short dark straight hair, plain light-grey crew-neck short-sleeve T-shirt, classic blue jeans, barefoot, stands at the edge of a high rocky cliff seen entirely from behind — his BACK to the camera, both hands clasped on the back of his head in shock and despair, looking out at the view. Before him and far below, the vast grey storm-churned sea stretches to the horizon and crashes white on the rocks — a dizzying drop, big waves, nothing but endless ocean. Dense tropical jungle frames the sides. We see the back of his head and body only, NOT his face. Soft flat overcast daylight, no sun, cool, not dark, wind in his clothes and hair. Overwhelming height, isolation and hopelessness, hyperrealistic, 8K.

pieces.caption: HELP! Somebody-- ...wait. What was that?
pieces.prompt: (swap clip: side-on, yells at sea, turns around, stares into the jungle)

pieces.caption: ...what the hell was that?!
pieces.prompt: 1:1 cinematic medium close-up portrait, shot straight from the FRONT. A Latino man with warm brown skin (clearly brown, not pale or white), short dark straight hair, plain light-grey crew-neck short-sleeve T-shirt, faces the camera directly with his head and shoulders square and upright — neck straight and anatomically normal, NOT twisted or turned. His face is frozen in pure spooked shock and fear — eyes wide and staring, eyebrows raised high, mouth open wide in a gasp — the unmistakable look of 'what the hell was that?!'. He is OUTDOORS on a clifftop: clearly visible BEHIND him is the open grey storm-churned SEA stretching to the horizon under a heavy overcast sky — an ocean-cliff background, definitely NOT a plain wall or indoor backdrop. Soft flat overcast daylight, no sun, cool desaturated tones, not dark, tense, ominous. Face sharp, prominent and clearly recognizable, hyperrealistic, 8K.
